for_planning_node_texts: Let me think about the arguments for abandoning marriage.
model_stance_conclusion_text: I think I lean towards disagreeing with abandoning marriage because the institution can be reformed to address its flaws while retaining the benefits.

nodes.node_text: Marriage has been around for a long time, right?

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage has been the foundation of the family unit for ages and abandoning it would contribute to the breakup of society.
marriage is a sacred concept that's been around throughout history; we can't just get rid of it.

marriage shouldn't be abandoned because it's a historical union between two people that has been going on for centuries. we cannot stop people from being married because our societal system is built around it
marriages have been a tradition for years, and it's a tradition that can go on forever.

nodes.node_text: It's a social and legal institution that formalizes relationships between people, usually for love, companionship, financial stability, and raising children.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.

nodes.node_text: Maybe some people argue that marriage is outdated because societal norms have changed.

argument_list.matched_argument: as society and values change, marriage is no longer as popular as it once was and divorce rates continue to climb.
marriage is an expensive and outdated institution that has failed to keep up with changing societal norms.
marriage is an outdated tradition that does not account for our changing society.
since it is an outdated concept. modern unions consist of many different types of couples who are unlikely to be life long partners and marriage creates a costly legal nightmare when they breakup.
We should abandon marriage as with divorce rates so high it is clear that is has become an outmoded concept.
we should abandon marriage because it's an outdated notion that in this day in age usually ends in divorce anyways.
with the divorce rates so high, marriage is pointless. and in recent times less and less people are even getting married. they just cohabitate and commit. marriage is becoming outdated.

nodes.node_text: Like, in modern times, people have more individual freedoms and might not need the institution to validate their relationships.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an outdated institution that has little meaning in today's society when it is perfectly acceptable for unmarried couples to live together.
marriage is antiquated so many people now live together as lifelong partners therefore marriage is an unnecessary act.
marriage is no longer necessary to gain the advantages of living as a couple
marriage is no longer necessary to obtain the benefits that have traditionally been associated with it
marriage is outdated in the modern era where there is noneed to make such a public statement of commitment in a relationship
marriage serves no purpose in this day and age as people are able to live together as couples anyway

nodes.node_text: Also, there's the issue of inequality in marriage.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage as a social institution is based on outdated notions of exchanging property and other resources in order to purchase or take ownership over a woman, and these practices should be abandoned.
marriage has become an outdated institution that rewards divorce settlements disproportionately for females giving them incentive to abuse the system.
marriage has roots in patriarchy and misogyny therefore it is time to move on to a new system.
marriage is a dated institution that unfairly benefits the patriarchy.
marriage is a social construct to oppress women & is backed by & enforced by the patriarchy

marriage is an archaic form of oppression against women and should be abandoned. it is used to tie a woman to a man to support the man in his success while diminishing the woman to a subservient role.
marriage is an institution founded on the outdated notion that women are property and should be discontinued
marriage is an institution that holds women down.
marriage is an outdated concept now that there is equality in the workplace which allows couples to have financial independence
marriage is an outdated institution for the benefit of the male population, therefore it should be abandoned.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of female ownership that has no place in a modern equal society.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of ownership of women that has no place in modern societies.
marriage is not necessary in todays society where ther are more rights and benefits for women
marriage just isn't necessary any more as male and female roles have changed tremendously.
marriage puts women in the subservient role and keeps them there.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.
some say that marriage is inherently misogynistic

nodes.node_text: Historically, marriage has been used to control women's rights, property ownership, and even sexual freedoms.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage as a social institution is based on outdated notions of exchanging property and other resources in order to purchase or take ownership over a woman, and these practices should be abandoned.
marriage has roots in patriarchy and misogyny therefore it is time to move on to a new system.
marriage is a dated institution that unfairly benefits the patriarchy.
marriage is a social construct to oppress women & is backed by & enforced by the patriarchy

marriage is an archaic form of oppression against women and should be abandoned. it is used to tie a woman to a man to support the man in his success while diminishing the woman to a subservient role.
marriage is an institution founded on the outdated notion that women are property and should be discontinued
marriage is an institution that holds women down.
marriage is an outdated concept which allowed fathers to sell their daughters and is still used in cultures to force women to marry their rapists
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of female ownership that has no place in a modern equal society.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of ownership of women that has no place in modern societies.
marriage puts women in the subservient role and keeps them there.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.
some say that marriage is inherently misogynistic

nodes.node_text: Maybe getting rid of marriage could eliminate some of these inequalities.

argument_list.matched_argument: "marriage" isn't keeping up with the times.  abandon the old thinking and bring something that incorporates all unions - not just those with a man and woman.
Marriage as a social institution is based on outdated notions of exchanging property and other resources in order to purchase or take ownership over a woman, and these practices should be abandoned.
marriage has roots in patriarchy and misogyny therefore it is time to move on to a new system.
marriage is a dated institution that unfairly benefits the patriarchy.
marriage is a social construct to oppress women & is backed by & enforced by the patriarchy

marriage is an archaic form of oppression against women and should be abandoned. it is used to tie a woman to a man to support the man in his success while diminishing the woman to a subservient role.
marriage is an institution founded on the outdated notion that women are property and should be discontinued
marriage is an institution that holds women down.
Marriage is an outdated institution. All types of relationships and family units (one-parent families, homosexual relationships, etc.) should be embraced and treated equally.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of female ownership that has no place in a modern equal society.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of ownership of women that has no place in modern societies.
marriage puts women in the subservient role and keeps them there.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.
some say that marriage is inherently misogynistic

nodes.node_text: Another point could be that marriage can be restrictive.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is confining and restricting and serves no purpose
Marriage restricts freedom. A person can't do as he or she pleases without considering the needs and wishes of his/her spouse.
we should abandon marriage it restricts the freedom of an individual

nodes.node_text: It's a lifelong commitment, and if a relationship doesn't work out, divorce can be messy and harmful.

argument_list.matched_argument: Abandoning marriage means no ugly divorce battle if things don't work out.
marriage is a contract assumed for life so when it fails, add to one or both parties in a state of depression and makes separation more traumatic
marriage is an outdated concept that does more harm than good.  most relationships don't work out forever, and making a person tied to another legally and financially forever is not fair.
Marriage is an outdated concept which cause emotional trauma when it doesn't work out and people have to dissolve it legally.
marriage traps people into a legal contract that they find hard to get out of if they decide later that it is not what was right for them.

marriage was started as a contract to support a wife and children.   in today's world many marriages fail causing a great cost to divorce,  it would be easier and less expensive not to have the contract at all.
marriage, weddings and divorces, they cost thousands and thousands and mean very little, often causing more stress and upheaval than happiness
since it is an outdated concept. modern unions consist of many different types of couples who are unlikely to be life long partners and marriage creates a costly legal nightmare when they breakup.
too many marriages now are falling apart.  if marriage was abandoned then it would be much easier to separate and less need for legal interference.
when a marriage breaks down it can cause a lot of stress and money to legally end it. it would be simpler if there was no marriage in place to start and both parties could just move on.

nodes.node_text: Maybe without marriage, people could have more flexible relationships without the pressure of permanence.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is antiquated. should be free to live in the kind of relationship based on their feelings and their commitment
marriage should be abandoned because if a couple wants to be together, they should not be required to be legally bound.

marriage tends to force people to remain in a relationship because of a piece of legal paper instead of having the freedom and ability to follow their own happiness which is vitally important for all involved.
We should abandon marriage as it just doesn't work anymore and people need to just understand that they are with someone for now and not have to feel committed for life.

nodes.node_text: Also, in some cultures, marriage is forced, leading to abuse or unhappiness.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an archaic religious institution and causes people to be stuck in abusive situations because they think divorce is wrong.
marriage is an outdated concept which allowed fathers to sell their daughters and is still used in cultures to force women to marry their rapists
Marriage keeps spouses stuck in abusive situations for longer than if they were not in a legal marriage relationship.

nodes.node_text: Abolishing it might reduce such issues.

argument_list.matched_argument: Abandoning marriage means no ugly divorce battle if things don't work out.
by adopting such practice we can get rid of all the burdens associate with marriage like financial, legal and social obligations.
marriage can be difficult to dissolve if the relationship breaks down. without marriage the couple will be able to get on with their separate lives much quicker.

marriage was started as a contract to support a wife and children.   in today's world many marriages fail causing a great cost to divorce,  it would be easier and less expensive not to have the contract at all.
people can live together and function without actually being married. makes things easier if they want to split up later.
there is no need for marriage in this society.  too many marriages end in divorce.  it would be easier to just get rid of that altogether.  safe money and stress.
too many marriages now are falling apart.  if marriage was abandoned then it would be much easier to separate and less need for legal interference.
when a marriage breaks down it can cause a lot of stress and money to legally end it. it would be simpler if there was no marriage in place to start and both parties could just move on.

nodes.node_text: On the counterarguments side, marriage provides legal and social benefits.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an important bond between people that offers financial and social benefits for those who choose to participate.
marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.

nodes.node_text: For example, inheritance rights, healthcare decisions, tax benefits, and immigration status.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage allows the legality of a spouse to be able to obtain medical information in the instance that a spouse is sick and/or hospitalized as well as acces to healthcare under a spouse's coverage plan.
marriage can show commitment and values it can give you rights and privileges under social security law and tax benefits
marriage is a legal relationship so you can claim certain benefits and get inheritance when your loved ones dies.
marriage is a straight-forward way to legalise a union and ensure arrangements such as inheritances and benefits are simplified.
marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.
the legality of marriage helps with health care and government benefits,

nodes.node_text: Without marriage, these could be harder to secure, especially for same-sex couples in places where it's not legally recognized.

argument_list.matched_argument: some cultures rely on marriage to be with their partners without being punished.

nodes.node_text: Also, marriage can offer stability and commitment, which are important for raising children.

argument_list.matched_argument: committment and stability are important in the lives of children and marriage provides these qualities
marriage provides a stable and secure home for the children and should not be abandoned.
marriage provides stability and a commitment between people which strengthens relationships and to abandon it would be to turn out backs on something good.

nodes.node_text: Studies might show that children from stable marriages do better in some aspects.

argument_list.matched_argument: children achieve better outcomes in life when their parents are married
children have better outcomes in life when their parents are married
Even with high rates of divorce, married couples and the children of married parents ultimately have more stable and emotionally healthy lives than their unmarried counterparts.
marriage is an important institution, it is well know that children who live with both birth  parents in a stable marriage succeed better in life, have better heath and better job prospects.
Marriage is associated with stable families; stable families produce happier children.
marriage is the base of the society. children growing up in married couples are more stable and not stressed than the non-married couples one
statistically parents who are married tend to stay together more than parents who are not married. this suggests that children will find greater stability in a house where the parents are married.

nodes.node_text: There's also the cultural and emotional aspect.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage often serves both culturally and religiously important purposes, and abandoning marriage altogether is likely impossible because of the deep ties it has in a persons identity and conception of the world.
marriage still has a place culturally and legally
marriage still has cultural and social significance, the joining of two people can truly bring happiness

nodes.node_text: Many people find value in the ceremony and the public commitment.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage is a meaningful way for a committed couple to show to the world and to each other their level of commitment, and to celebrate their relationship.
marriage is the ultimate show of committment between people and many people value the ritual

nodes.node_text: It can strengthen a relationship by providing a framework for shared responsibilities.

argument_list.matched_argument: if there was no legal marriage process, family stability would no longer be possible and a the word "responsibility" would become instinct.
marriage contributes a solild framework and structure to a relationship, gives legal representation and stability.
marriage provides stability and a commitment between people which strengthens relationships and to abandon it would be to turn out backs on something good.

nodes.node_text: Plus, marriage can offer a sense of belonging and community support.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage is a way to bring families together and to grow the web of support that one has in their lives by creating these bonds that are incredibly meaningful to people.
marriage provides stability and a commitment between people which strengthens relationships and to abandon it would be to turn out backs on something good.

nodes.node_text: Wait, but some might counter that these benefits could be achieved through other legal agreements without the institution of marriage.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is no longer necessary since other legal arrangements are now available
marriage is no longer necessary to obtain the benefits that have traditionally been associated with it

the government should have no say so in people's love lives. marriage is a government institution. the same things achieved by marriage can be achieved without that piece of paper and government involvement.

nodes.node_text: Cohabitation agreements, civil unions, or other forms of partnership could provide the legal protections without the traditional marriage structure.

argument_list.matched_argument: far too many marriages end now in divorce, or worse; civil unions offer almost the same  benefits as marriage anyway.
Marriage is a fundamentally religious practice at its basis, and therefore the state should not officially sanction marriage in any way, but instead have other legal mechanisms for civil partners.
marriage is no longer necessary since other legal arrangements are now available
marriage is no longer necessary to obtain the benefits that have traditionally been associated with it
marriage is out dated and should be replaced by an all-encompassing partnership status that everyone can access
marriage is outdated and truly offers no benefits in this society.  it should be abandoned and laws be put in effect to mimic the few benefits it has financially.

the government should have no say so in people's love lives. marriage is a government institution. the same things achieved by marriage can be achieved without that piece of paper and government involvement.

nodes.node_text: So maybe the problem isn't marriage itself but the lack of alternatives or the rigidity of the institution.

argument_list.matched_argument: "marriage" isn't keeping up with the times.  abandon the old thinking and bring something that incorporates all unions - not just those with a man and woman.
marriage is out dated and should be replaced by an all-encompassing partnership status that everyone can access
we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.

nodes.node_text: Another angle: the economic aspect.

argument_list.matched_argument: by adopting such practice we can get rid of all the burdens associate with marriage like financial, legal and social obligations.
getting marriage is very expensive and divorce can be very expensive too.
marriage and the accompanying wedding imposes massive financial hardship at the start of a relationship, often contributing to its eventual dissolution.
Marriage and weddings are often incredibly costly affairs that ultimately are a poor use of fungible resources, as they serve to further no end and have no productive outcome.

Marriage ceremonies/wedding receptions can be incredibly expensive for both attendees and hosts. The money spent on weddings could be better used elsewhere, such as towards a couple's new home, or for some charitable cause.
marriage costs people thousands, only for them to divorce again later in most cases nowadays. if marriage was abandoned people would save a fortune.
marriage is costly and money save by abandoning it could be used for other things
marriage is nothing more than a piece of paper that creates financial problems.

marriage was started as a contract to support a wife and children.   in today's world many marriages fail causing a great cost to divorce,  it would be easier and less expensive not to have the contract at all.
most marriages end in divorce, which causes an economic hardship on both parties.  it makes more financial sense to not get married in the first place
There are many financial benefits associated with marriage.

nodes.node_text: Marriage can pool resources, which is beneficial, but maybe that's more about financial planning than the institution itself.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage allows for increased economic freedom due to the combined incomes
marriage as a financial and social construct still has much merit and is as popular now as ever.
Marriage makes things easier for couples for many financial and legal matters.
marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.
There are many financial benefits associated with marriage.

nodes.node_text: Also, in some cases, marriage can lead to economic dependency, which might not be healthy.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is out dated now as people who marry rarely stay together for life you are also responsible foryour spouse so could be responsible for their debts

nodes.node_text: Marriage has evolved over time—like, it used to be more about property and family alliances, but now it's more about love and companionship.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriages are sacred and traditional, even though the definitions can evolve over time.
while it has sketchy beginnings,  marriage has become an important step for commitment for those in serious relationships. it is a massive commitment that proves love.

nodes.node_text: So maybe instead of abandoning it, we should reform it instead of getting rid of it entirely.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is a wholesome, normal act that teaches family values.  we shouldn't be abandoning the concept but rather fear why more marriages are failing these days and fix it.
we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.

nodes.node_text: What about the argument that marriage perpetuates gender roles?

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage just isn't necessary any more as male and female roles have changed tremendously.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.

nodes.node_text: Traditional marriages often have expectations about who does what (like the man being the breadwinner), which can be oppressive.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.

nodes.node_text: But again, this is more about changing the institution rather than abolishing it.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is a wholesome, normal act that teaches family values.  we shouldn't be abandoning the concept but rather fear why more marriages are failing these days and fix it.
we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.

nodes.node_text: Also, the emotional security aspect.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is a way to help people feeling loved and secure
to many, marriage is incredibly important and provides security and a family foundation

nodes.node_text: Some people feel that the commitment of marriage gives them a sense of security and purpose.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage gives the couple assurance they ate committed to eachother.
marriage is a sacred union that establishes security for families in society. it ensures that couples and families have supportive relationships to build a life upon.
marriage is a way to help people feeling loved and secure

marriage is important to many people. a lot of people have dreams and goals of marrying. it protects those in the relationship. it provides assurance. why not allow those who do want to marry, the choice.
to many, marriage is incredibly important and provides security and a family foundation

nodes.node_text: Without it, they might feel less secure in their relationships.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is a way to help people feeling loved and secure

marriage is important to many people. a lot of people have dreams and goals of marrying. it protects those in the relationship. it provides assurance. why not allow those who do want to marry, the choice.

nodes.node_text: If we abandon marriage, would the legal benefits be replaced by other systems?

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is no longer necessary since other legal arrangements are now available
marriage is no longer necessary to gain the advantages of living as a couple
marriage is no longer necessary to obtain the benefits that have traditionally been associated with it
marriage is outdated and truly offers no benefits in this society.  it should be abandoned and laws be put in effect to mimic the few benefits it has financially.

nodes.node_text: Maybe, but it might take time and effort.
The inequality and control aspects are serious, but again, reform could address those.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is a wholesome, normal act that teaches family values.  we shouldn't be abandoning the concept but rather fear why more marriages are failing these days and fix it.
we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.

nodes.node_text: The restrictiveness of lifelong commitment is a problem, but maybe people can choose different types of relationships without needing an institution.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an outdated institution and if a couple wish to spend the rest of their lives together, then they are free to do so with or without marriage.
marriage is an outdated institution that simply binds people by a piece of paper and has no more or less committment than a couple who would choose to live together.
marriage is antiquated and not necessary for couples who are committed to each other.
marriage is antiquated. should be free to live in the kind of relationship based on their feelings and their commitment
marriage is just a piece of paper than can be nullified at any given time. people can live together without being married just the same.
marriage is just a piece of paper, people can love and support each other the same no matter what.
marriage is only piece of paper which couples get when they got married and people do not need that piece of paper to live together and marriage should be abandoned.
marriage is outdated and we don't need to make it official to really love someone
marriage is unnecessary when two people love each other. being together should be enough.
marriage serves no purpose in this day and age as people are able to live together as couples anyway
people can live together and function without actually being married. makes things easier if they want to split up later.

the government should have no say so in people's love lives. marriage is a government institution. the same things achieved by marriage can be achieved without that piece of paper and government involvement.
we should abandon marriage because in the world we live in today does not need marriage.  many people live together and are perfectly happy without being married.
you don't not need a piece of paper to be with a person throughout life

nodes.node_text: Alternatively, maybe the institution itself isn't the problem but how it's structured.

argument_list.matched_argument: we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.

nodes.node_text: So instead of abandoning it, we should make it more flexible, allow for easier dissolution, or remove gendered roles.

argument_list.matched_argument: we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.

nodes.node_text: If the core issues can be addressed through reform, then maybe abandoning isn't necessary.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is a wholesome, normal act that teaches family values.  we shouldn't be abandoning the concept but rather fear why more marriages are failing these days and fix it.
we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.

nodes.node_text: However, if the institution is inherently flawed and resistant to change, then maybe abolition is better.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an out date institution, and promotes failure in the form of divorce, so we should abandon it.
The institution of marriage has deteriorated over the last century and has little value aside from legal benefits. It should be cast aside.
with divorce rates up so high, we should abandon marriage as an archaic and broken tradition.

nodes.node_text: 1. **Outdated Norms and Inequality**:      Marriage has historically reinforced patriarchal structures, limiting women’s rights and perpetuating gender roles (e.g., expectations of women as caregivers).

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage as a social institution is based on outdated notions of exchanging property and other resources in order to purchase or take ownership over a woman, and these practices should be abandoned.
marriage has roots in patriarchy and misogyny therefore it is time to move on to a new system.
marriage is a dated institution that unfairly benefits the patriarchy.
marriage is a social construct to oppress women & is backed by & enforced by the patriarchy

marriage is an archaic form of oppression against women and should be abandoned. it is used to tie a woman to a man to support the man in his success while diminishing the woman to a subservient role.
marriage is an institution founded on the outdated notion that women are property and should be discontinued
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of female ownership that has no place in a modern equal society.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of ownership of women that has no place in modern societies.
marriage puts women in the subservient role and keeps them there.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.
some say that marriage is inherently misogynistic

nodes.node_text: It has also excluded marginalized groups, such as LGBTQ+ individuals, until recently.

argument_list.matched_argument: "marriage" isn't keeping up with the times.  abandon the old thinking and bring something that incorporates all unions - not just those with a man and woman.
Marriage is an outdated institution. All types of relationships and family units (one-parent families, homosexual relationships, etc.) should be embraced and treated equally.

nodes.node_text: Abolishing marriage could dismantle these systemic inequalities.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage as a social institution is based on outdated notions of exchanging property and other resources in order to purchase or take ownership over a woman, and these practices should be abandoned.
marriage has roots in patriarchy and misogyny therefore it is time to move on to a new system.
marriage is a dated institution that unfairly benefits the patriarchy.
marriage is a social construct to oppress women & is backed by & enforced by the patriarchy

marriage is an archaic form of oppression against women and should be abandoned. it is used to tie a woman to a man to support the man in his success while diminishing the woman to a subservient role.
marriage is an institution founded on the outdated notion that women are property and should be discontinued
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of female ownership that has no place in a modern equal society.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of ownership of women that has no place in modern societies.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.

nodes.node_text: 2. **Restrictiveness and Lack of Flexibility**:      Marriage often implies permanence and exclusivity, which can trap individuals in unhappy or abusive relationships.

argument_list.matched_argument: legally requiring people to stay together is ridiculous
Marriage can extend the time that couples stay together in a bad or unhealthy relationship because they fear the stigma of a divorce.
marriage is an archaic religious institution and causes people to be stuck in abusive situations because they think divorce is wrong.
marriage is an outdated concept that does more harm than good.  most relationships don't work out forever, and making a person tied to another legally and financially forever is not fair.
marriage is confining and restricting and serves no purpose
Marriage keeps spouses stuck in abusive situations for longer than if they were not in a legal marriage relationship.
Marriage restricts freedom. A person can't do as he or she pleases without considering the needs and wishes of his/her spouse.

marriage tends to force people to remain in a relationship because of a piece of legal paper instead of having the freedom and ability to follow their own happiness which is vitally important for all involved.
marriage traps people into a legal contract that they find hard to get out of if they decide later that it is not what was right for them.
we should abandon marriage it restricts the freedom of an individual

nodes.node_text: The pressure to "commit forever" may stifle personal growth or lead to divorce-related trauma.

argument_list.matched_argument: abandoning marriage allows for people to grow as themselves without the weight of a relationship

Marriage as an institution places pressure on couples in long term relationships that often creates tension between the individuals and lures people into getting married when they aren't ready, ruining otherwise good relationships.
marriage is a contract assumed for life so when it fails, add to one or both parties in a state of depression and makes separation more traumatic
marriage is an outdated concept that does more harm than good.  most relationships don't work out forever, and making a person tied to another legally and financially forever is not fair.
Marriage is an outdated concept which cause emotional trauma when it doesn't work out and people have to dissolve it legally.

marriage tends to force people to remain in a relationship because of a piece of legal paper instead of having the freedom and ability to follow their own happiness which is vitally important for all involved.

nodes.node_text: Alternatives like cohabitation or open relationships might offer more autonomy.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an outdated institution that has little meaning in today's society when it is perfectly acceptable for unmarried couples to live together.
marriage is antiquated. should be free to live in the kind of relationship based on their feelings and their commitment
Marriage is over rated and now many people just prefer to live together.
with the divorce rates so high, marriage is pointless. and in recent times less and less people are even getting married. they just cohabitate and commit. marriage is becoming outdated.

nodes.node_text: 3. **Cultural and Economic Coercion**:      In some cultures, marriage is forced, leading to exploitation or loss of autonomy.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is a forced union society seems to think is needed to make a family and indicate loyalty to a significant other. marriage is not needed to prove love.
marriage is an outdated concept which allowed fathers to sell their daughters and is still used in cultures to force women to marry their rapists
Marriage keeps spouses stuck in abusive situations for longer than if they were not in a legal marriage relationship.

nodes.node_text: Additionally, economic dependency within marriage can disadvantage those who lack financial independence, particularly in cases of divorce.

argument_list.matched_argument: given that so many marriages end in divorce now and many people who stay together only because they cannot afford a divorce it is time marriage was abandoned.
marriage is an outdated concept that does more harm than good.  most relationships don't work out forever, and making a person tied to another legally and financially forever is not fair.
marriage provides protection for couples. it gives them rights with regards to property meaning that if the marriage breaks down one person cannot take everything and leave the other destitute.

nodes.node_text: 4. **Legal Protections Can Be Decoupled**:      Benefits like healthcare rights, inheritance, and tax breaks could be extended through civil unions or legal agreements without the institution of marriage.

argument_list.matched_argument: far too many marriages end now in divorce, or worse; civil unions offer almost the same  benefits as marriage anyway.
Marriage is a fundamentally religious practice at its basis, and therefore the state should not officially sanction marriage in any way, but instead have other legal mechanisms for civil partners.
marriage is no longer necessary since other legal arrangements are now available
marriage is no longer necessary to obtain the benefits that have traditionally been associated with it
marriage is outdated and truly offers no benefits in this society.  it should be abandoned and laws be put in effect to mimic the few benefits it has financially.

the government should have no say so in people's love lives. marriage is a government institution. the same things achieved by marriage can be achieved without that piece of paper and government involvement.

nodes.node_text: This would allow for more inclusive and flexible frameworks.

marriage is out dated and should be replaced by an all-encompassing partnership status that everyone can access

nodes.node_text: 1. **Legal and Social Benefits**:      Marriage provides critical legal protections (e.g., hospital visitation rights, shared finances, immigration sponsorship) that are harder to secure without formal recognition.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage allows the legality of a spouse to be able to obtain medical information in the instance that a spouse is sick and/or hospitalized as well as acces to healthcare under a spouse's coverage plan.
marriage can show commitment and values it can give you rights and privileges under social security law and tax benefits
marriage is a legal relationship so you can claim certain benefits and get inheritance when your loved ones dies.
marriage is a straight-forward way to legalise a union and ensure arrangements such as inheritances and benefits are simplified.
marriage is an important bond between people that offers financial and social benefits for those who choose to participate.
marriage is an important safeguard in terms of legal and financial binding.
Marriage makes things easier for couples for many financial and legal matters.
marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.

some people want to get married and have the legal protections and benefits that marriage provides. others are required to do so for religious reasons. nobody who doesn't want to get married is required to
the legality of marriage helps with health care and government benefits,

nodes.node_text: Removing marriage could disproportionately harm vulnerable groups, such as same-sex couples in regions without robust alternatives.

argument_list.matched_argument: some cultures rely on marriage to be with their partners without being punished.

nodes.node_text: 2. **Stability for Families and Children**:      Research often links stable marriages to better outcomes for children, including emotional security and economic stability.

argument_list.matched_argument: children achieve better outcomes in life when their parents are married
children have better outcomes in life when their parents are married
committment and stability are important in the lives of children and marriage provides these qualities

it provides a stable environment for children
marriage brings stability to the family unit which children need to develop into proper citizens of a society.
marriage has shown to create a better environment to raise kids.
marriage is an important institution that can provide a secure foundation for raising children.
marriage is an important institution, it is well know that children who live with both birth  parents in a stable marriage succeed better in life, have better heath and better job prospects.
Marriage is associated with stable families; stable families produce happier children.
marriage is the base of the society. children growing up in married couples are more stable and not stressed than the non-married couples one
marriage is the ideal stable relationship to raise children and helps people to live longer more stable lives.

nodes.node_text: While not universal, the institution can foster commitment and shared responsibility in raising families.

committment and stability are important in the lives of children and marriage provides these qualities

marriage is a sacred union that establishes security for families in society. it ensures that couples and families have supportive relationships to build a life upon.

nodes.node_text: 3. **Emotional and Cultural Significance**:      Many people derive meaning from the public commitment of marriage, which reinforces emotional bonds and community support.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage is a meaningful way for a committed couple to show to the world and to each other their level of commitment, and to celebrate their relationship.
Marriage is a way to bring families together and to grow the web of support that one has in their lives by creating these bonds that are incredibly meaningful to people.

Marriage often serves both culturally and religiously important purposes, and abandoning marriage altogether is likely impossible because of the deep ties it has in a persons identity and conception of the world.

nodes.node_text: 4. **Potential for Reform Over Abolition**:      Rather than abandoning marriage, its flaws can be addressed through legal reforms (e.g., no-fault divorce, gender-neutral roles, prenuptial agreements).

we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.
